normalized_title: State Planning Policy 2.6 - State Coastal Planning Policy
version_date: Gazetted 30 July 2013 (replaces the SPP 2.6 gazetted 10 June 2003 as amended 19 December 2006)
scope_summary: State-wide policy for land use and development in the Western Australian coastal zone. It sets policy measures for coastal development and settlement, building height limits within 300 m of the shoreline, coastal hazard risk management and adaptation planning (CHRMAP), infill development, coastal protection works and coastal foreshore reserves, with Schedule One prescribing the methodology for calculating coastal process (erosion and inundation) allowances over a 100-year planning timeframe. It applies to scheme-making, strategies, structure plans, subdivision and development on or affecting the coast.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: all development except industrial/resource development, transport, telecommunications, engineering infrastructure and port works
coastal hazard risk assessment and coastal process allowances
S3 erosion and inundation calculations for all coastal development
cross-shore storm erosion on a typical sandy coast
added to S1+S2+S3 erosion allowances on sandy coasts
sandy coast erosion allowance; zero where accretion is less than 0.2 m/yr
steeply sloping sandy shorelines absent geological assessment
lots in vulnerable coastal areas - 'VULNERABLE COASTAL AREA' notification re erosion/inundation over the next 100 years
